title: Termux: AAudio input stream fails to open at runtime
description:
Field report: lingot-tuner compiles and links on Termux but Core::start returns Err -> 'failed to start audio: ...'.

Traced: Config::default sets audio_device=None, so the default_input_device() path is taken. cpal's aaudio backend returns Device(None) for that, and pick_config resolves from a hardcoded table (default_supported_configs) which cannot fail. So the Err originates in build_input_stream or play() — AAudio refusing to open a recording stream.

Leading hypothesis: the Termux app lacks RECORD_AUDIO. Android permission checks are per-UID, and Termux add-ons share sharedUserId com.termux, so installing Termux:API and granting it the mic permission is the usual way to make native recording work from the shell. UNVERIFIED — needs the actual error text from the device.

NEXT STEP: get the exact string after 'failed to start audio: '. A CLI hint pointing at RECORD_AUDIO has been added, but the root cause is not confirmed.

Note also: cpal on android only has the AAudio host. Running Termux's pulseaudio package will NOT help, since the pulse backend is not compiled for the android target.
close_reason: CONFIRMED in the field on a Pixel 10a: the cause was the Android microphone permission, as hypothesised. Granting Termux RECORD_AUDIO makes lingot-tuner-cli capture and detect pitch. Termux:API was already installed in this case, so its role (declaring RECORD_AUDIO + sharing the UID) is a prerequisite users may need to satisfy separately — the hint now tells them to CHECK for it rather than assuming it is present. Hint text deduplicated into lingot_tuner::audio_start_hint(); README gained an Android/Termux section.

title: 4 pre-existing clippy warnings in the lingot lib; blocks a -D warnings CI job
description: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets emits 4 warnings, all in the lingot library: clone() on SupportedStreamConfigRange which is Copy (audio.rs ~200); an if with identical blocks (signal.rs ~197); a loop variable only used to index 'pole' (filter.rs ~175); FftPlan has a public len() but no is_empty() (fft.rs ~49). Exit code is 0 so they are non-fatal, but a clippy job with -D warnings would fail immediately. Clear these, then add the job.

title: Add ratatui terminal frontend (lingot-tuner-tui) for Termux
description:
winit gates x11_platform on free_unix, which excludes android_platform, so the egui GUI can never run under Termux-X11 — a terminal gauge is the only native option. Added lingot-tuner-tui behind an optional (default-on) 'tui' feature; ratatui is pure Rust + termios and checks clean for aarch64-linux-android.

Also extracted needle smoothing from gui.rs into gauge.rs (Needle) so both frontends share one damped-spring filter, and split advance_by(target, dt) out of advance() so the motion is deterministically testable — the original clock-reading API made a tight test loop advance zero simulated time.

Verified: renders correctly in a pty and tracks live pitch from a real mic on desktop (D4 293.60 Hz, -0.4 cents, in tune). NOT yet verified on an actual Termux device — audio capture there is still broken (see lingot-tuner-rust-0bk).
close_reason: Landed in 0.2.1: lingot-tuner-tui + shared gauge::Needle. Verified rendering and live pitch on desktop; on-device Termux verification still blocked by 0bk.

title: Verify on a real Termux device that test binaries link and pass
description:
The dev box can only 'cargo check' for aarch64-linux-android — there is no Android linker here, so the link step is unverified. The open question is cpal: its Android backend goes through ndk-sys, which links against libaaudio (/system/lib64/libaaudio.so). Termux's clang may not have that on its library search path by default.

Steps: on Termux, pkg install rust, then run
  cargo test --workspace
  cargo build --workspace --no-default-features   # CLI only

If linking against libaaudio fails, the fallback is to put cpal behind an optional 'audio' feature in the lingot crate and split lingot-tuner::core so Decimator/Analyzer (pure DSP, 4 tests) stay ungated — but do that only if it actually fails; per the hard rule cpal is NOT exempt.

title: input_devices() panicked on Termux; release notes overclaimed test coverage
description:
lingot::audio::input_devices() called cpal's host.input_devices(), whose android backend enters JNI via ndk_context::android_context() -> expect() -> PANIC when no JavaVM is registered (the normal state for a Termux CLI process). cpal guards the call with 'if let Ok(..)', which a panic bypasses.

Consequence: the unit test listing_input_devices_does_not_panic — whose whole purpose is asserting no panic — would have aborted the test binary on Termux. The v0.2.0 hard rule and release notes claim 'cargo test passes on Termux'; that was verified with 'cargo check --target aarch64-linux-android' only, never by running tests on a device. The claim was unverified and likely false.

Fixed by routing enumeration through enumerate_input_devices(), which catch_unwind's the panic and treats an un-enumerable host as 'no named devices'. Opening the default device never needed enumeration.

FOLLOW-UP: the hard rule in CLAUDE.md says tests must PASS on Termux, but the enforceable local gate is only a compile check. Either run tests on a device before making that claim again, or weaken what the rule claims to guarantee.
close_reason: input_devices() now routes through enumerate_input_devices(), which catch_unwind's the JNI panic. Shipping in 0.2.1.

title: Bead history is not actually versioned: .beads/issues.jsonl is an absolute symlink
description:
CORRECTED. The original claim in this bead was FALSE and is retained here as a record of the error.

WRONG: 'all bead history exists only in the local beads store and is lost if that machine is.'

ACTUAL: bd keeps its canonical store on an independent git branch inside this repo, refs/heads/beads/store (state.jsonl, deps.jsonl, tombstones.jsonl, meta.json), and auto-pushes it. Verified: refs/heads/beads/store and refs/remotes/origin/beads/store are the same SHA, so bead data was versioned and on origin the whole time and is recoverable by any clone.

The REAL and much smaller problem: nothing human-readable appeared on main, because .beads/issues.jsonl is a symlink into ~/.local/share/beads-rs/ and git stored only the path string.

Why it cannot simply be a regular file: beads-rs 0.1.26 src/compat/export.rs has ensure_symlinks(), which renames any regular .beads/issues.jsonl to issues.jsonl.bak and recreates the symlink. Confirmed empirically. No config can redirect the export; the path is hardcoded to data_dir()/exports/<sha256(remote)[:16]>/issues.jsonl.

FIX: gitignore bd's own paths and commit a separate .beads/issues.export.jsonl, refreshed by .beads/refresh-export.sh. Verified to survive bd's next sync unchanged. Residual cost: the export does not self-update, so run the script before committing bead changes.
close_reason: Fixed via committed export (.beads/issues.export.jsonl + refresh-export.sh), and the bead's original premise corrected — bd already versioned its store on refs/heads/beads/store and had pushed it.

title: cargo fmt --check fails across the tree; not enforced in CI
description: The workspace has apparently never been rustfmt'd. 'cargo fmt --check' exits 1 with diffs in ~10 files (audio.rs, config.rs, fft.rs, filter.rs, scale.rs, signal.rs, window.rs, core.rs, gui.rs, tui.rs). A fmt job was deliberately left out of the new CI workflow because it would be red on day one. Fixing means one large mechanical reformat commit, after which the CI job can be added.

title: Keep everything but the GUI compilable on Termux (aarch64-linux-android)
description: Standing hard rule, now in CLAUDE.md. eframe/winit pulls android-activity, which fails to compile for aarch64-linux-android. Fixed by declaring eframe under [target.'cfg(not(target_os = "android"))'.dependencies] and gating lingot_tuner::gui + main.rs on not(target_os="android"). cpal builds fine on Android via the ndk/AAudio backend. Verified: cargo check --target aarch64-linux-android --workspace --all-targets passes with default features; cargo test --workspace still 42 green natively.
close_reason: eframe moved to a cfg(not(target_os=android)) target dependency; gui module and GUI main gated; android check + native tests both green.

title: Vendor upstream sources under vendor/, cloned and gitignored
description: Standing hard rule 2 in CLAUDE.md. The original C lingot was being referenced as ../lingot/src/ — a path outside the repo that only resolves on the machine that set it up. Now: git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/ibancg/lingot.git vendor/lingot, with vendor/ in .gitignore. CLAUDE.md intro + reference-files table updated to vendor/lingot/src/. vendor/ is read-only reference material, is not part of the build, and is exempt from the Termux rule.
close_reason: vendor/lingot cloned from upstream, vendor/ gitignored, CLAUDE.md hard rule 2 + reference paths updated.

title: Add CI job running the aarch64-linux-android check gate
description: CLAUDE.md now mandates 'cargo check --target aarch64-linux-android --workspace --all-targets' before every commit. That is currently enforced only by convention. Add it to CI (rustup target add aarch64-linux-android + the check) so a dependency that breaks the Android target is caught automatically rather than on someone's phone.
